data.title: Yomiuri: Japan Govt to be Able to Order Drug Firms to Increase Production if Shortage Occurs
data.description:
By Mizuki Arimura

Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writer

The Japanese government will be authorized to order pharmaceutical companies to increase production or imports of prescription drugs that are essential for the country's residents when there is a shortage of such drugs.

The framework will authorize the Health, Labor and Welfare Ministry to give such orders from Thursday and will cover 75 ingredients, including those used to make antibiotics that treat serious illnesses. The ministry will also be able to disclose the names of companies that fail to comply with the orders.

The health ministry aims to establish a system that ensures a stable drug supply to medical institutions.

Certain active ingredients of prescription drugs, which are used to treat serious illnesses and used by many patients, have been designated as "drugs that require a stable supply." Regarding such drugs, the ministry has been providing financial assistance to pharmaceutical companies to cover such costs as finding suppliers of active ingredients and increasing production to resolve shortages.

The revised Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Law, which was enacted by the Diet in May, will be implemented on Thursday. In line with the implementation, the ministry will add 256 ingredients to the list of drugs that require a stable supply, increasing the total to 762. The 75 ingredients, which include those used to make antibiotics, anesthetics, immunosuppressants, antipyretic analgesics and anticoagulants, were selected from the list of 762.

When the new framework goes into effect, pharmaceutical companies will be obliged to report to the health ministry if they plan to suspend or restrict shipments of prescription drugs.

If there is a shortage of at least one of the 75 ingredients, or if the possibility of a shortage is extremely high, the ministry can order pharmaceutical companies to increase production or imports. Pharmaceutical companies will be required to submit and implement plans for increasing production, such as hiring more personnel or utilizing other production lines, which are normally used to make different products, to make those that are in short supply.

Companies will also be required to develop and implement plans to prevent drug shortages during normal times, such as securing multiple suppliers and building stockpiles. The health ministry will be authorized to disclose the names of companies that fail to comply without justifiable reasons.

In 2019, there was a shortage of an essential antibiotic in Japan due to problems in manufacturing an active ingredient in China, threatening surgeries. Since 2021, shortages of various prescription drugs have become a significant issue, triggered by quality control fraud committed by generic drugmakers.

The health ministry also plans to establish a fund to subsidize costs of generic drug manufacturers' efforts to collaborate with other companies to boost productivity, such as fees for building and upgrading facilities, as early as Thursday. The ministry aims to encourage such companies to enhance their manufacturing capacity.

Through the use of the framework and the fund, the health ministry hopes to prevent a situation in which essential drugs are in short supply.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-M0000159190-1

data.title: USD/JPY Could Reach 157.60 Amid Policy Speculation — Market Talk
data.description: The yen could reach as weak as 157.60 against the dollar Thursday amid receding expectations for an immediate rate hike by the Bank of Japan and doubts about a December rate cut by the U.S. Fed, says Sony Financial analyst Kumiko Ishikawa. "Profit-taking is likely at the high end of the dollar-yen range, but if the results [of September U.S. jobs data due later in the day] are stronger than market expectations, the market may further diminish December rate hike speculation, potentially leading to a stronger dollar," she adds. USD/JPY is last at 156.92. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: Sigma Healthcare Initiated with A$3.40/Share Price Target by UBS
James Hardie Price Target Cut 19% to A$30.50/Share by UBS
Three new listings contribute to 10% of NSE cash turnover on Wednesday amidst listing frenzy
data.description:
Dalal Street’s newest entrants stole the show on Wednesday, November 19, contributing to over 10% of the NSE’s total cash market turnover as investors continued to chase the listing frenzy across the new entrants.

Tenneco Clean Air India, Billionbrains Garage Ventures, and Physicswallah together accounted for 10.2% of the NSE’s nearly ₹1 lakh crore cash turnover, underscoring the sustained appetite for newly listed companies, even as investors chose to take some money off the table after strong listings, particularly in the latter two stocks.

In its debut session, Tenneco Clean Air India Ltd. outpaced even the bluest of blue chips, topping the NSE’s turnover chart with trades worth ₹5,101 crore. The US-based Tenneco Group–backed auto components maker surged as much as 30.2% in intra-day trade from its IPO price of ₹397 after raising ₹3,600 crore through the three-day bidding process.

Close behind was Billionbrains Garage Ventures, the parent of discount broker Groww, which logged ₹2,522 crore in trades. Shares of the trading platform, which had rocketed nearly 90% in five sessions, hit its first lower circuit on Wednesday, trimming activity and pulling its average turnover since listing down to ₹6,100 crore.

Ed-tech firm Physicswallah rounded out the trio, generating ₹2,369 crore in turnover as 16 crore shares changed hands. This marked a cooldown from its stunning debut-day turnover of ₹4,124 crore, with the stock ending 7.7% lower at ₹143.28.

Interestingly, while Tenneco held onto its debut gains, both Groww and Physicswallah ended the day in the red, signaling early profit-booking after a strong listing day performance.

data.title: Oakland, Calif., to Sell $334 Million of General Obligation Bonds
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

The City of Oakland, Calif., plans to sell $334 million of general obligation bonds to pay for capital projects and affordable housing initiatives.

The sale includes tax-exempt and taxable securities that will be sold in multiple tranches, according to documents posted Tuesday on MuniOS. Pricing is expected on Dec. 3 and closing is set for Dec. 18.

The offering consists of $94 million of tax-exempt Series 2025B-1 GO bonds, $180 million of federally taxable Series 2025B-2 social bonds, $10 million of federally taxable Series 2025B-3 GO bonds, and $48 million of tax-exempt Series 2025B-4 refunding bonds. All interest from the securities are exempt from California state taxes.

The bonds will be backed by taxes levied by the Oakland City Council.

Proceeds from the offering will provide funds for the acquisition improvement, construction and repair of affordable housing.

Other planned uses include street repaving, curb ramp and sidewalk reconstruction, bridge rehabilitation and streetscape improvements. Money will be used to improve local parks, libraries and senior centers. Money from the sale will also be used to refund previously issued bonds and to pay issuance costs.

Interest will be paid starting on Jan. 15 and again on June 15.

Moody's assigned the bonds a rating of Aa2 with a negative outlook. S&P Global Ratings gave the bonds an AA- rating and also assigned them a negative outlook.

Siebert Williams Shank is senior manager on the sale.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: Nikkei Rises 3.3% After Nvidia's Strong Earnings Soothe Investors' Nerves — Market Talk
data.description: The Nikkei Stock Average rises 3.3% to 50150.56 in early trade after Nvidia's strong earnings soothe investors' nerves. With AI bubble fears growing over the last few weeks and putting pressure on tech stocks, the markets and tech stocks got a "pop the champagne moment" with Nvidia's robust earnings and guidance, Wedbush analysts say in a research note. Among the best performers on the Japanese benchmark index, Renesas Electronics climbs 5.1%, JX Advanced Metals adds 4.9%, and Yaskawa Electric is 4.5% higher. USD/JPY is at 156.92 compared with 157.15 late Wednesday in New York.(ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Lenovo 2Q Revenue Rises on Strong AI Push
data.description:
By P.R. Venkat

Lenovo Group's revenue rose sharply in the second quarter, benefiting from the global push to adopt Windows 11 and the world's largest PC maker's growing server business amid the artificial-intelligence boom.

Revenue for the three months ended Sept. 30 increased 15% from a year earlier, to $20.45 billion, marginally topping market expectation of $20 billion.

Net profit fell 5% on year, to $340 million, missing the $382 million expected in a FactSet poll of analysts.

However, its adjusted net profit, excluding non-cash and non-operating effects, rose 25%, to $512.0 million.

Lenovo's main business, the intelligent-devices segment that includes PCs, smartphones and tablets, posted a 15% rise in revenue in the first half.

The global PC market has maintained strong momentum, helped by the Windows 11 transition.

Shipments of PCs in the September quarter rose 17%, to 19.4 million units, with a market share of 25.5%, according to IDC data.

Write to P.R. Venkat at venkat.pr@wsj.com

data.title: BEACN Announces 2025 Q3 Results and New CEO
data.description:
Not for distribution to United States Newswire Services or for dissemination in the United States

VICTORIA, BC / ACCESS Newswire / November 19, 2025 / BEACN Wizardry & Magic Inc. ("BEACN" or the "Company") announces financial results for the quarter ending September 30, 2025, as well as the appointment of Kevin Alexander as Chief Executive Officer effective November 19th, 2025. Co-founder and former Chief Executive Officer Craig Fraser will continue with the company full-time as Chief Product Officer.

In the first nine months of 2025, BEACN realized a 16% decrease in revenue and a 17% decrease in gross profits when compared to the same period last year. Gross margin was maintained at 42% in the first nine months of 2025 (2024 - 42%) due to product mix and price increases which were offset by increased merchant fees and product discounts. In Q3 2025, BEACN realized a 24% decrease in sales and a 7% decrease in gross profit when compared to the same period last year. The reduction in sales and gross profit compared to the same period last year is attributed to no in-sell of new product in Q3 2025 vs Q3 2024 (BEACN Studio introduction). Removing in-sale figures from 2024, D2C revenue was down 5% vs. the same period last year. Weaker D2C sales are attributed to full implementation of increased USD retail prices across the product range due to US Tariffs. Further information is available from the Company's financial statements for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, available under the Company's profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

BEACN further announces Kevin Alexander will transition from his role as a consultant and Director with BEACN to Chief Executive Officer ("CEO"). Kevin has spent many years in leadership positions where creating successful products, marketing and customer experience content were a constant necessity. This change comes with Craig Fraser resigning as CEO and accepting the role of Chief Product Officer ("CPO"). The co-founders and board of directors agreed this allows Craig the time to focus on product and customer experience while Kevin leads strategy and drives growth.

"I, along with the all the directors are confident this is the right time for this change. Kevin thrives in bringing teams together, and I have worked with him in achieving challenging goals with agility and speed in the past" says Craig Fraser, BEACN CPO and co-founder.

Kevin Alexander adds "It has been a privilege to support BEACN this far. Getting to start in my new role already having experience with the team and business means we do not have to skip a beat. I look forward to helping the BEACN team to focus on what they are great at for this next stage of BEACN's growth." These changes are effective November 19th, 2025. Both Craig and Kevin continue their role as BEACN Board Members.

About BEACN


BEACN , a Victoria BC based consumer electronics company, develops innovative audio equipment, peripherals and technology for gamers, live streamers, and content creators. BEACN is committed to delivering premium products that enable everyone to produce studio-quality content. BEACN's award-winning product ecosystem includes BEACN Mic, BEACN Studio, BEACN Mix and BEACN Mix Create. BEACN is listed on the TSXV under the symbol BEACN.

Media & Investor Enquiries

Liberty Brunet

ir@beacn.com

These forward-looking statements include, among other things, statements relating to: (a) [B2B Retail Strategy]; (b) [Online, Direct to Consumer Sales Strategy]; (c) [Product Design & Introduction]; and (d) [Quarterly Earnings].

Such forward-looking statements are based on a number of assumptions of management, including, without limitation: (a) [Company's cost and timing expectations are accurate]; (b) [Company will be successful in the deployment of its resources and personnel]; (c) [Company's ability to maintain manufacturing volume for its products and its ability to sustain sales of products to customer]; and (d) [general economic and market conditions will not change in an adverse manner; political and regulatory stability including in financial and capital markets ].

data.title: Nikkei Stock Average Extends Gains; Last Up 3.0% at 49983.76
KMD Brands Yet to Convince Tide Has Turned — Market Talk
data.description: There was a lot to like about KMD Brands' 1Q update, but Forsyth Barr isn't convinced that the tide has turned with key trading periods ahead. KMD reported an 8% rise in 1Q sales, albeit at a 120 bps lower gross margin than a year earlier. Analyst Paul Laxton Koraua says KMD sold older inventory at clearance prices to reduce debt and create space for new products. That's sensible given KMD's elevated net leverage ratio, Forsyth Barr says. "The trading update also proved that KMD can generate sales at the right price, albeit at a 1Q gross margin of 55.6%, which we think the group would be unlikely to sustain for the entire year," Forsyth Barr says. It retains a neutral call on the stock. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)
